root_cause: Race condition in ensure_mcp_initialized(). The method was a sync fn that fired a tokio::spawn background task, then run() immediately read self.mcp — which was still None because the background task had not yet acquired the write lock and completed initialization. The fix converts ensure_mcp_initialized to an async fn that runs inline (not in a spawned task), so MCP servers are fully connected and tools are registered before run() reads self.mcp at lines 305-317 of stack_run.rs.

details.broken_flow: run() calls ensure_mcp_initialized() [sync, no-await] → tokio::spawn(background task) → run() immediately reads self.mcp (still None) → per-run registry built with zero MCP tools
details.fixed_flow: run() calls ensure_mcp_initialized().await [async, inline] → MCP servers connect → tools registered → self.mcp populated → run() reads self.mcp (has runtime) → per-run registry built with MCP tools
details.secondary_note: The per-run registry in stack_run.rs correctly reads from self.mcp (lines 305-317) — this code was not broken. The problem was purely that self.mcp was always None when that code executed.

changes.mod.rs: ensure_mcp_initialized converted from sync fn (tokio::spawn) to async fn (inline await). Disabled-set is now properly read via .await instead of the previous empty-HashSet workaround.
changes.stack_run.rs: self.ensure_mcp_initialized() changed to self.ensure_mcp_initialized().await at line 122.

test_output: mcp.weather.weather appears in tool list when running: cargo run --bin ava -- 'List all available tools' --headless --provider openrouter --model moonshotai/kimi-k2.5 --max-turns 2
